description: tqdm is a fast, extensible progress meter for Python and CLI.

documentation.example: from tqdm import tqdm; [x for x in tqdm(range(100))]
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/tqdm && python3 tqdm.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/tqdm && python3 tqdm.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/tqdm && python3 tqdm.py --help`. Fast, extensible progress meter for loops.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the utility category. Fits when the engagement needs: Fast, extensible progress meter for loops.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Fast, extensible progress meter for loops.

metadata.example: from tqdm import tqdm; [x for x in tqdm(range(100))]

name: tqdm
summary: Fast, extensible progress meter for loops.

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.
